name: Komodo Dragon
summary: Komodo dragons are the largest living lizards. They live on a few Indonesian islands, hunt large prey with a powerful bite, and use their forked tongues to track food by smell.
metaTitle: Komodo Dragon Facts | Habitat, Diet, Size & Photos
metaDescription: Discover Komodo dragon facts with photos, island habitat, carnivore diet, hatchlings, and conservation. Learn where Komodo dragons live and why they are endangered.
searchIntents: how big is a komodo dragon

lifespan.captivity: Up to 30 years or more in zoos

speed: Up to 12 mph in short bursts
funFacts: Komodo dragons are the largest lizards alive today.
They can smell carrion from miles away using their forked tongues.
Young Komodo dragons often live in trees to avoid being eaten by adults.
A Komodo dragon can run faster than many people expect for such a large reptile.
Komodo dragons can swim between nearby islands when they need to move.
Female Komodo dragons can sometimes lay fertile eggs even without a male.

faq.question: What is a Komodo dragon?
faq.answer: A Komodo dragon is a huge monitor lizard and the largest living lizard on Earth. It lives on a few islands in Indonesia.

faq.question: Where do Komodo dragons live?
faq.answer: Wild Komodo dragons live only on a few Indonesian islands, mainly in and around Komodo National Park.

faq.question: What do Komodo dragons eat?
faq.answer: Komodo dragons are carnivores. They hunt deer, wild pigs, and other large animals, and they also eat carrion.

faq.question: Are Komodo dragons endangered?
faq.answer: Yes. Komodo dragons are listed as Endangered because they live in a very small wild range and face habitat and prey loss.

faq.question: Are Komodo dragons venomous?
faq.answer: Yes. A Komodo dragon bite delivers venom and harmful bacteria that help weaken prey.

faq.question: How big is a Komodo dragon?
faq.answer: Large adults can grow up to about 10 feet long and weigh around 150 to 200 pounds.
